behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "generated function signature matches the expected interface ... specified in the description." Slot B: model-output. Slot C: If lowered to Integration with stubbed outputs, you can only verify parser/validator wiring, and miss real LLM mis-generation of names/types/return annotations. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior asks for one generation quality check, not a required multi-step agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real LLM on a fixed prompt set with explicit interface constraints; compile/inspect produced signatures and assert conformance on first attempt output.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt." Slot B: handoff across local modules (orchestrator, test runner adapter, bookkeeping). Slot C: If lowered to Unit, you miss ordering/short-circuit bugs at module boundaries (e.g., runner result not propagated, extra attempt still fired). Integration brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic stubs for generator and test runner: attempt 1 fails, attempt 2 passes, attempt 3 should never run. Assert call order, stop condition, and returned attempt metadata.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts." Slot B: multi-step agentic (generate -> test feedback -> refine -> retest with real model behavior). Slot C: If lowered to Agentic single-step, you miss failures in cross-attempt state carryover and retry-loop convergence that only appear across sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real LLM on a benchmark function known to fail often on first try; require eventual pass within <=3 attempts and verify each attempt consumed prior failure context.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt." Slot B: handoff from test-result module to prompt-builder module. Slot C: If lowered to Unit, you can miss schema/serialization mismatches where runner outputs are not correctly transferred into prompt fields. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because this behavior checks prompt content construction, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic failed test result objects from runner output into orchestration path and assert next prompt payload contains exact failed test identifiers and messages.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context." Slot B: multi-step agentic (at least two model generations linked by failure feedback). Slot C: If lowered to Agentic single-call checks, you miss loop-level regressions where context is built but not actually used across attempts, producing duplicate code. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because deterministic stubs cannot validate that real model behavior changes due to steering context.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 -> fail tests -> build refinement prompt -> live attempt 2; assert attempt-2 code is materially different and addresses referenced failure points (not exact replay).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results." Slot B: handoff/state aggregation across orchestrator and result formatter. Slot C: If lowered to Unit, you miss integration bugs where attempt history is truncated/misaligned when assembled from runner outputs. Integration brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess and no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic failing attempts (stub generator + stub runner) and assert: exactly 3 executions, termination after third fail, and returned error object contains ordered attempt artifacts and test-result details.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full non-agentic pipeline wiring ... with deterministic stubbed model outputs." Slot B: handoff across multiple local modules with stubs. Slot C: If lowered to Unit, you miss cross-module contract failures between generation adapter, execution, and bookkeeping. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text explicitly scopes to non-agentic deterministic stubbed outputs and does not require real OS/network/subprocess effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline with deterministic generator stubs and controlled test outcomes; validate generated artifact routing, runner invocation, attempt counters, and final state assembly.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves ... hard function that fails in the first attempt." Slot B: multi-step agentic sequence with convergence requirement. Slot C: If lowered to Agentic, you can test one model output quality but miss orchestration-plus-model interaction needed for iterative recovery after initial failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated hard-function prompt where first attempt is expected to fail; run real loop and require eventual pass in later attempt with preserved attempt trail and feedback-driven refinement.
